title: Airframe Structures Engineer - Commercial Aircraft OEM
setting: A large airframe program designing and sustaining primary structure
companySize: Structures team of 40-100 inside a program of 1,000+ people
description: You work on the parts of the aircraft that simply cannot fail: wing structure, fuselage frames, fittings, and load paths. The day moves between analysis, drawings, stress review, concession questions, and long conversations about margins.

schedule.activity: Review overnight analysis runs and supplier questions on structure changes
schedule.note: A small geometry change can ripple into multiple load cases.

schedule.activity: Attend a cross-functional design review with loads, materials, and manufacturing teams
schedule.note: The best structural decisions are rarely made by the structures team alone.

schedule.activity: Update a local structural model and check stress or deflection margins
schedule.note: You are usually asking whether the margin is real, not just whether it is positive.

schedule.activity: Respond to a manufacturing concession or repair assessment
schedule.note: Production realities constantly test the neatness of the original design.

schedule.activity: Write up structural rationale for a design review or certification package
schedule.note: Documentation quality matters when structure becomes evidence.

schedule.activity: Plan the next analysis or test needed to retire open structural risk
schedule.note: You rarely leave with every load case fully closed.

responsibilities: Assess structural margins for critical components
Coordinate with loads, materials, and manufacturing teams
Interpret FEA results and turn them into decisions
Contribute to fatigue and certification evidence
challenges: Managing uncertainty in simplified structural models
Balancing weight reduction against margin and manufacturability
Resolving urgent production or supplier issues without weakening long-term design integrity
rewards: Owning work that directly affects aircraft safety
Seeing abstract load paths become real hardware decisions
Becoming fluent in a highly respected engineering discipline
careerPath: Graduate stress analyst -> Structures engineer -> Senior stress engineer -> Structures lead -> Chief structures engineer
